name: Precoro Purchase Order and Procurement API
what_it_does: Cloud-based procurement and purchase order management platform with REST API — enabling SMB and mid-market companies to automate purchase orders, invoice processing, expense management, and approval workflows, with integrations to accounting systems (QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite) and a user-friendly interface designed for non-procurement professionals.
use_cases: Automating purchase order creation and approval workflows via Precoro API for procurement digitization
Syncing Precoro purchase orders and invoices with accounting systems (QuickBooks, Xero) via API
Querying spend analytics and budget tracking data via Precoro API for finance reporting
Managing supplier catalog and vendor information via Precoro API for supplier data governance
Processing invoice approvals and 3-way matching via Precoro API for accounts payable automation
not_for: Complex strategic sourcing and RFx (use Jaggaer or Coupa for complex sourcing events)
Very small businesses with simple needs (use QuickBooks directly for basic purchase tracking)
Enterprise-scale procurement with complex global workflows (use SAP Ariba or Coupa)
best_when: A growing SMB or mid-market company (50-500 employees) needs to replace spreadsheets and email with a structured procurement system — with quick setup, ease of use, and integration to existing accounting software.
avoid_when: You're enterprise scale with complex procurement, already have SAP/Oracle procurement modules, need advanced sourcing event management, or have highly complex approval hierarchies.

auth.notes: API key from Precoro account settings. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped access. Webhook for procurement event notifications (PO created, approved, invoice received). REST API for procurement workflow automation. Integration with QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Slack. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — API key provides full account access.

pricing.paid_tiers: From $35/user/month; Small Team plan from $499/month (up to 20 users); contact sales for larger teams
pricing.notes: Ukrainian company (Kyiv). Founded 2015. Bootstrapped and profitable. Remote-first team. Strong focus on ease of use for non-procurement professionals — key differentiator vs. complex enterprise tools. QuickBooks, Xero, and NetSuite integrations make it popular for accounting-first companies. 1,000+ customers. Competitive with Procurify and Tradogram. Ukrainian company maintained operations through 2022 Russian invasion — demonstrated resilience.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests per minute; contact support for limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for procurement workflow automation. API key bearer auth. Good documentation with clear examples. Webhook for procurement events. QuickBooks/Xero/NetSuite integration documented. Rate limits partially documented (100 req/min). Ukrainian company with EU data residency option. No OAuth2 — simpler than enterprise alternatives.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Procurement reads fully idempotent. PO reads idempotent by PO number. Invoice reads idempotent. PO creation requires external reference for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Ukrainian company — evaluate geopolitical risk for critical procurement infrastructure
No OAuth2 — API key provides full account access without scope granularity
Rate limit (100 req/min) documented but enforcement details unclear
Integration with accounting systems (QuickBooks/Xero) is via API — requires separate configuration
Not designed for complex enterprise procurement — scope to SMB/mid-market use cases

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. US and EU data residency. API key auth. Ukrainian company with GDPR awareness. Procurement data is sensitive (purchase orders, invoices, supplier terms) — restrict API key access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. API key provides full account access. Geopolitical risk as Ukrainian company — assess for critical infrastructure.
